channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Trump taps Keith Kellogg as special envoy to Ukraine and Russia
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday announced his appointment of Keith Kellogg, a retired lieutenant general who served as chief of staff on Trump’s National Security Council during his first term, as special envoy to Ukraine and Russia.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Trump taps retired General Keith Kellogg for Ukraine envoy role
item.description: While there is currently no special envoy for the war in Ukraine, Trump had privately expressed interest in creating the position.
item.dc:creator: Gram Slattery, Svea Herbst-Bayliss and Steve Holland / Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: US issues new sanctions on Maduro allies in Venezuela over fraudulent elections
item.description: The Biden administration is sanctioning nearly two dozen Venezuelan security and Cabinet officials aligned with President Nicolás Maduro, building on a series of sanctions it has imposed along with other countries following July elections the U.S. deems illegitimate.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: China releases 3 'wrongfully detained' Americans, White House says
item.description: China is releasing three Americans who were "wrongfully detained," a National Security Council spokesperson says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fugitive on FBI's 'Most Wanted Terrorist' list captured after more than 20 years on run
item.description: Daniel Andreas San Diego, a fugitive on the FBI’s “Most Wanted Terrorist” list, was captured in the United Kingdom on Monday after more than 20 years.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Biggest November snowstorm in half century hits Seoul and grounds flights
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The biggest November snowstorm to hit South Korea’s capital in more than a half century blanketed the capital on Wednesday, grounding hundreds of flights, disrupting commuter traffic and leaving at least two dead.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about the ceasefire deal between Israel and Lebanon's Hezbollah
item.description: A ceasefire deal that went into effect on Wednesday could end more than a year of cross-border fighting between Israel and Lebanon's Hezbollah militant group, raising hopes and renewing difficult questions in a region gripped by conflict. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: ADAM GELLER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: ICC prosecutor requests arrest warrant for the head of Myanmar's military regime
item.description: The International Criminal Court’s chief prosecutor has asked judges to issue an arrest warrant for the head of Myanmar’s military regime for crimes committed against the country’s Rohingya Muslim minority.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Thousands of Lebanese return to their homes as Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire takes hold
item.description: Thousands of Lebanese displaced by the war between Israel and Hezbollah returned home Wednesday as a ceasefire takes hold.
item.dc:creator: Kareem Chehayeb And Tia Goldenberg, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pakistan ends lockdown of its capital after Imran Khan supporters are dispersed by police
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Authorities reopened roads linking Pakistan's capital with the rest of the country, ending a four-day lockdown, on Wednesday after using tear gas and firing into the air to disperse supporters of imprisoned former Prime Minister Imran Khan who marched to Islamabad to demand his rele
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Philippine police file criminal complaints against Sara Duterte and her security aides
item.description: Philippine police officials have filed criminal complaints against Vice President Sara Duterte and her security staff for allegedly assaulting authorities and disobeying orders in a recent altercation in Congress.
item.dc:creator: Jim Gomez, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Australian father of teen sextortion victim backs banning young children from social media
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Wayne Holdsworth became an advocate for banning Australian children younger than 16 from social media because his son took his own life after falling victim to an online sextortion scam.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah in Lebanon begins
item.description: A ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon’s Hezbollah militants began early Wednesday morning, after Beirut residents endured the most intense day of Israeli strikes since the war began. Many wondered if the agreement to stop fighting would hold.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An Australia police officer who shocked a 95-year-old woman with a Taser is guilty of manslaughter
item.description: A police officer who shocked a 95-year-old nursing home resident with a Taser was found guilty of manslaughter in an Australian court.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Australia's plan to ban children from social media proves popular and problematic
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — How do you protect children from the harms of social media? Politically, the answer appears simple in Australia, as legislation tackling the problem moves quickly through the Parliament. Practically, however, the solution could be far more difficult.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: World's oldest man has died in England at 112
item.description: John Alfred Tinniswood was born a few months after the sinking of the Titanic and lived through two world wars.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Philippine investigators summon VP Duterte over her public threats against President Marcos
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Philippine authorities handed a subpoena to Vice President Sara Duterte’s office Tuesday, inviting her to answer investigators' questions after she publicly threatened to have the president, his wife and the House of Representatives speaker assassinated if she were killed
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Childhood cancer patients in Lebanon must battle disease while under fire
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Carol Zeghayer gripped her IV as she hurried down the brightly lit hallway of Beirut’s children’s cancer center. The 9-year-old's face brightened when she spotted her playmates from the oncology ward.
item.dc:creator: SALLY ABOU AL JOUD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fugitive on FBI's 'Most Wanted Terrorist' list captured in UK after 20 years
item.description: Daniel Andreas San Diego, a fugitive on the FBI’s “Most Wanted Terrorist” list, was captured in the United Kingdom on Monday after more than 20 years. San Diego, 46, wanted for his alleged involvement in two bombings in Northern California in the 2000s, was arrested in Wales, the U.K.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Russian migrants being denied access to asylum process: attorney
item.description: "They are being subjected to a blanket policy that they are applying to Russian asylum seekers," he said.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine says Russian attack sets a new record for the number of drones used
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia launched 188 drones against most regions of Ukraine in a nighttime blitz, the Ukrainian air force said Tuesday, describing it as a record number of drones deployed in a single attack.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel ramps up strikes on Beirut as its leadership prepares to vote on ceasefire with Hezbollah
item.description: The Israeli military also issued warnings for 20 more buildings in Beirut's suburbs to evacuate before they too were struck
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Bangladeshi court denies bail to Hindu leader who led rallies for minority protection
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — A prominent Bangladeshi Hindu leader who has been leading rallies demanding security for Hindus in the overwhelmingly Muslim nation was ordered detained on charges of sedition Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top EU diplomat says Israel has 'no excuses' to refuse a ceasefire with Hezbollah
item.description: FIUGGI, Italy (AP) — The European Union’s top diplomat said Tuesday there were “no excuses” for Israel to refuse to accept a ceasefire with the Lebanese militant group Hezbollah, saying all its security concerns had been addressed in the U.S.-French-brokered deal.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump, Congress looking to put suffocating sanctions on 'kangaroo' ICC over Netanyahu arrest warrant
item.description: After the ICC’s legal assault on the Mideast’s only democracy, Israel, for its war on Hamas terrorism, a powerful U.S. legislative and executive campaign has emerged against the controversial body.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russian forces capture former British soldier fighting for Ukraine in Kursk: report
item.description: A former British Army soldier was reportedly captured by Russian forces while fighting alongside Ukrainian soldiers in the Kursk region of the country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel launches new airstrikes on Lebanon as leaders draw closer to a ceasefire with Hezbollah
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Israel's military launched airstrikes across Lebanon on Monday, unleashing explosions throughout the country and killing at least 31 while Israeli leaders appeared to be closing in on a negotiated ceasefire with the Hezbollah militant group.
item.dc:creator: SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, JOSEF FEDERMAN and SAM METZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump pledges Mexico, Canada, China tariffs over border, fentanyl
item.description: "Thousands of people are pouring through Mexico and Canada, bringing Crime and Drugs at levels never seen before," Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Bah, humbug! Vandal smashes Ebenezer Scrooge's tombstone used in 'A Christmas Carol' movie
item.description: If life imitates art, a vandal may be haunted by The Ghost of Christmas Yet to Come for smashing a tombstone that marked the fictional grave of Ebenezer Scrooge in the English countryside.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hezbollah fires barrage of rockets into Israel after IDF targets Hezbollah command centers in Beirut
item.description: Hezbollah on Sunday fired missiles and other projectiles into Israel in retaliation for to deadly IDF strikes on the militant group’s command centers in Beirut.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine to analyze fragments of missile fired by Russia capable of carrying nuclear warheads
item.description: Ukrainian investigators allowed journalists to view remains of an intermediate-range ballistic missile used by Russia on the battlefield, before an analysis could be completed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Shootout near Israeli Embassy in Jordan leaves 3 police officers injured, gunman dead
item.description: Police in Jordan shot and killed a gunman who wounded three police officers near the Israeli Embassy on Sunday morning, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli strikes in central Beirut kill at least 20 as diplomats push for a cease-fire
item.description: BEIRUT, Lebanon (AP) — Israeli airstrikes Saturday in central Beirut killed at least 20 people, officials said, as the once-rare attacks on the heart of Lebanon's capital continued without warning while diplomats scrambled to broker a cease-fire.
item.dc:creator: ABBY SEWELL and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Justin Trudeau dances at Taylor Swift concert amid destructive riot in Montreal, sparking outrage
item.description: Video of Justin Trudeau dancing at a Taylor Swift concert sparked outrage over the weekend after a destructive Montreal protest took place on the same night.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu ‘in very bad company’ with ICC indictment: William Cohen
item.description: The ICC issued arrest warrants for Netanyahu and a former defense minister on war crimes allegations.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Top war-crimes court issues arrest warrants for Netanyahu and others in Israel-Hamas fighting
item.description: THE HAGUE (AP) — The world’s top war-crimes court issued arrest warrants Thursday for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, his former defense minister and Hamas’ military chief, accusing them of crimes against humanity in connection with the 13-month war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Ukraine says Russia launched an intercontinental missile in an attack for the first time in the war
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said a missile used “matches the speed and altitude” of an ICBM. The Kremlin refused to comment on the strike.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Arhirova, WGN 9
